title: tibouchina heteromalla
intro: Tibouchina heteromalla, also known as Alena, is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, native to Brazil and Argentina. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Tibouchina heteromalla include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat wounds, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Tibouchina heteromalla can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
